title: Shorting the semis
question: Does conditioning a semiconductor short on "overbought" (RSI>70, >15% above 200-DMA, +20%/21d) beat shorting blind, and does the answer depend on size?
finding: No. Across 68 chip names over ten years the overbought short earns a negative edge over a blind short at every horizon (5/21/63/126d: -0.15 / -0.40 / -1.93 / -4.29pp). Size doesn't save it (negative for >$500B and <$10B; the only positive bucket is 4 names); a genuinely selective filter (all-three, 3.5% of days) is the worst short of all; the null is worse out of sample and unchanged by borrow cost. Overbought is a melt-up warning, not a short signal.
